pattern: Each position's requirement (prime at even indices, non-prime at odd indices) only depends on that one element, so incrementing every element independently up to the nearest value satisfying its own requirement and summing those increments is optimal.
hints: Since increasing one element never affects any other element's primality, every index can be solved completely independently of the rest.
For an even index, find the smallest value at least as large as the current one that is prime; for an odd index, find the smallest value at least as large as the current one that is NOT prime.
Because gaps between primes stay small at these magnitudes, a simple upward search testing each candidate with trial division reaches the target quickly; sum the differences across every index.

edge_inputs.description: both an even and an odd index need raising
the array already satisfies the alternating pattern
only the even index needs a single increment
a single-element array only needs its one even-position value to become prime
the value 1 is not prime, so an odd index holding 1 already satisfies its requirement with zero cost
an odd index sitting on a prime just below a long run of primes needs several increments to reach a non-prime
the largest allowed value at the final position

stress.description: random array across the full length and value bound
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
